Contract Safety Management (CSM) system at BPCL Refinery
Detail Process Description
Contractor prequalification and selection based on safety aspects
i. Assessment during bidding stage
Contractors will be prequalified based on their safety competency assessment during the bidding
stage itself. There are six competency measurements identified for evaluation with each
competency carrying a weightage as below
Sl. No Competency Weightage
1 Manpower 15
2 Tools and Tackles 20
3 Safety Records 20
4 Similar jobs executed 15
5 Safety accreditations 16
6 Safety practices 14
Total 100
The contractor has to fill in only Annexure 1 & 2 forms indicating whether they comply with
our requirements or not. All the supporting documents to be attached with Annexure 1 & 2
and return the forms along with other bid documents. Details of documents to be submitted
as given in “Contractor Safety Evaluation Scoring System” sheet for ease of reference.
Based on the indications and details filled in the safety competency forms, each bidder will be
assessed by an Evaluation Committee. Those bidders securing less than 50 points out of 100
points will be considered as disqualified & not safety competent and their offer will be
rejected at the bidding stage (Technical and Commercial evaluation will not be done)
The details of assessment to be carried out by the Evaluation Committee on safety competency
form is attached as Annexure 3
ii. Assessment during work execution stage
The Contractors Safety Performance (CSP) Report (Annexure 4) shall be provided to the
successful bidder along with the contract document for onsite evaluation. The Contractor shall
delegate a representative /himself / safety officer of the contractor to ensure the compliance with
the CSP. This shall be strictly adhered to by the contractor without fail during the execution of the
contract. Review of the CSP report and compliance shall be ensured by the Engineer-in-Charge
breadcrumb: TITLE OF TENDER: ……………………………………………………………………………… > ANNEXURE > ii. Assessment during work execution stage

content:
(EIC) of the Job. Approved CSP report shall be submitted to the Safety section on a monthly basis
for on- the-job evaluation. The safety section will carry out the job evaluation based on the CSP
submitted by the contractor.
In case of noncompliance with respect to any points in the CSP, the same shall be brought to the
notice of the contractor and letter/mail for compliance shall be issued by the Engineer-in- Charge
of the job. It shall also be intimated to the contractor that noncompliance to withP/failure to
provide CSP on monthly basis shall lead to the application of penalty to the contractor as per
Safety and Security regulations of BPCL
iii. Final Safety Assessment
On completion of the job, the evaluation committee shall assess the contractor on a 100-point
scale based on the pre-job evaluation and on the job performance. The final safety assessment
document indicating the contractor’s safety performance prepared by the evaluation committee
shall be forwarded to the EIC of the job. EIC shall submit the same along with the final bill during
final bill settlement.
The final safety assessment document indicating the contractor’s safety performance shall be a
mandatory document which needs to be submitted along with the final bill. The final safety
assessment format is given as attachment as Annexure 5.
The details of assessment to be carried out by the Evaluation Committee on safety competency
form during the job execution stage and final safety assessment are attached as Annexure 6
iv. Holiday listing
a. If any of the information given by the bidder in the safety competency form is found to be
wrong or misleading at the scrutiny stage or at a later stage, it will be treated as a violation of
contract terms and suitable action such as holiday listing shall be initiated based on the
recommendation from the evaluation committee as per Guidelines for procurement of goods and
contract, Clause no.4.2.12 Policy for holiday listing of vendor in BPCL (Sl. No.2 – Submitted fake,
false or forged documents/ certificates – 3 years)
b. Contractors scoring less than 50 points in the final safety assessment shall be rated poor
and holiday listed for future tenders based on the recommendation from the evaluation
committee as per Guidelines for procurement of goods and contract, Clause no.4.2.12 Policy for
holiday listing of vendor in BPCL (Sl. No.8 – Poor performance of the contract or has abandoned
the contract – 1 year)
Note: Annexures 4, 5 & 6 are given herewith only for information. Annexures 4 & 5 are not
required to be submitted along with the bid. These annexures are required to be filled out
and submitted during the execution of job by successful L1 bidder only.

Major Risk Potential The following types of contractor work are generally considered to be major risk
potential:
1. Contractor performing maintenance or repair, turnaround, major
renovation, of process equipment or specialty work on or adjacent to a
process unit and whose work can impact process safety / fire more than 15
minutes, property loss more than Rs 10 Lacs
2. Contractors whose work could result in fatality. Examples include:
Electrical work
Hot work
Height Work
High pressure water blasting
Elevated work
Demolition
Scaffold erection/dismantling
Excavation
Cranes near power lines
Construction
Confined space entry including entries into inert atmospheres
Abrasive blasting
Pressure Testing
Serious Risk Potential This type of contractor provides general services and support general maintenance.
They have low potential of impacting process safety, potential for exposure to
significant health risks and high potential for exposure to significant personnel safety
risks, e.g.:
Building and grounds (lawn care, painting, janitorial, paving, pest
control, canteen, plumbing, etc.)
Inspection
Laborers (stores, reproduction)
Vehicle mechanics
Contractors whose work could result in Reportable Lost Time Injuries
Minor Risk Potential Contractors providing clerical or technical expertise and knowledge in support of
plant and business operations (Technical & Administrative Contractors) generally
have minimal exposure to significant safety and health risks. Technical and
administrative contractors generally perform their work in an office environment or
are escorted by Location representatives while in the operating units.

Detail Process Description
Contractor prequalification and selection based on safety aspects
i. Assessment during bidding stage
Contractors will be prequalified based on their safety competency assessment during the bidding
stage itself. There are six competency measurements identified for evaluation with each
competency carrying a weightage as below
Sl. No Competency Weightage
1 Manpower 15
2 Tools and Tackles 20

4 Similar jobs executed 15

The contractor has to fill in only Annexure 1 & 2 forms indicating whether they comply with
our requirements or not. All the supporting documents to be attached with Annexure 1 & 2
and return the forms along with other bid documents. Details of documents to be submitted
as given in “Contractor Safety Evaluation Scoring System” sheet for ease of reference.
Based on the indications and details filled in the safety competency forms, each bidder will be
assessed by an Evaluation Committee. Those bidders securing less than 50 points out of 100
points will be considered as disqualified & not safety competent and their offer will be
rejected at the bidding stage (Technical and Commercial evaluation will not be done)
The details of assessment to be carried out by the Evaluation Committee on safety competency
form is attached as Annexure 3
ii. Assessment during work execution stage
The Contractors Safety Performance (CSP) Report (Annexure 4) shall be provided to the
successful bidder along with the contract document for onsite evaluation. The Contractor shall
delegate a representative /himself / safety officer of the contractor to ensure the compliance with
the CSP. This shall be strictly adhered to by the contractor without fail during the execution of the
contract. Review of the CSP report and compliance shall be ensured by the Engineer-in-Charge

content:
(EIC) of the Job. Approved CSP report shall be submitted to the Safety section on a monthly basis
for on- the-job evaluation. The safety section will carry out the job evaluation based on the CSP
submitted by the contractor.
In case of noncompliance with respect to any points in the CSP, the same shall be brought to the
notice of the contractor and letter/mail for compliance shall be issued by the Engineer-in- Charge
of the job. It shall also be intimated to the contractor that noncompliance to withP/failure to
provide CSP on monthly basis shall lead to the application of penalty to the contractor as per
Safety and Security regulations of BPCL
iii. Final Safety Assessment
On completion of the job, the evaluation committee shall assess the contractor on a 100-point
scale based on the pre-job evaluation and on the job performance. The final safety assessment
document indicating the contractor’s safety performance prepared by the evaluation committee
shall be forwarded to the EIC of the job. EIC shall submit the same along with the final bill during
final bill settlement.
The final safety assessment document indicating the contractor’s safety performance shall be a
mandatory document which needs to be submitted along with the final bill. The final safety
assessment format is given as attachment as Annexure 5.
The details of assessment to be carried out by the Evaluation Committee on safety competency
form during the job execution stage and final safety assessment are attached as Annexure 6
iv. Holiday listing
a. If any of the information given by the bidder in the safety competency form is found to be
wrong or misleading at the scrutiny stage or at a later stage, it will be treated as a violation of
contract terms and suitable action such as holiday listing shall be initiated based on the
recommendation from the evaluation committee as per Guidelines for procurement of goods and
contract, Clause no.4.2.12 Policy for holiday listing of vendor in BPCL (Sl. No.2 – Submitted fake,
false or forged documents/ certificates – 3 years)
b. Contractors scoring less than 50 points in the final safety assessment shall be rated poor
and holiday listed for future tenders based on the recommendation from the evaluation
committee as per Guidelines for procurement of goods and contract, Clause no.4.2.12 Policy for
holiday listing of vendor in BPCL (Sl. No.8 – Poor performance of the contract or has abandoned
the contract – 1 year)
Note: Annexures 4, 5 & 6 are given herewith only for information. Annexures 4 & 5 are not
required to be submitted along with the bid. These annexures are required to be filled out
and submitted during the execution of job by successful L1 bidder only.

Major Risk Potential The following types of contractor work are generally considered to be major risk
potential:
1. Contractor performing maintenance or repair, turnaround, major
renovation, of process equipment or specialty work on or adjacent to a
process unit and whose work can impact process safety / fire more than 15
minutes, property loss more than Rs 10 Lacs
2. Contractors whose work could result in fatality. Examples include:
Electrical work
Hot work
Height Work
High pressure water blasting
Elevated work
Demolition
Scaffold erection/dismantling
Excavation
Cranes near power lines
Construction
Confined space entry including entries into inert atmospheres
Abrasive blasting
Pressure Testing
Serious Risk Potential This type of contractor provides general services and support general maintenance.
They have low potential of impacting process safety, potential for exposure to
significant health risks and high potential for exposure to significant personnel safety
risks, e.g.:
Building and grounds (lawn care, painting, janitorial, paving, pest
control, canteen, plumbing, etc.)
Inspection
Laborers (stores, reproduction)
Vehicle mechanics
Contractors whose work could result in Reportable Lost Time Injuries
Minor Risk Potential Contractors providing clerical or technical expertise and knowledge in support of
plant and business operations (Technical & Administrative Contractors) generally
have minimal exposure to significant safety and health risks. Technical and
administrative contractors generally perform their work in an office environment or
are escorted by Location representatives while in the operating units.
